zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William la Zouche於1321年12月25日出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯時，他的父親Eudes la Zouche24歲，母親Joan Inge23歲。他於1337年7月16日與Elizabeth de Ros在英國諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1382年4月23日在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯享年60歲，埋葬在英國巴金哈姆爾比德萊斯登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William la Zouche于1321年12月25日出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯时，他的父亲Eudes la Zouche24岁，母亲Joan Inge23岁。他于1337年7月16日与Elizabeth de Ros在英国諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1382年4月23日在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯享年60岁，埋葬在英国巴金哈姆爾比德萊斯登。

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Zouche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse, Chantal, Dominique, Francois, Herve, Michel, Rodolphe, Serge.
French (Fouché): variant of Foucher . Compare Fooshee , Foshee , Fouch , and Foushee .
Altered form (Fouché) of French Foucher (compare 1 above), found mainly in South Africa.

variants.name: Foucher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Etienne, Henri, Lydie, Marcel, Rosaire.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Folchari, composed of the elements folk, folc ‘people’ and hari ‘army’. Compare Faucher 2.

variants.name: Foushee
meanings: History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its original form Fouché) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Fouch
meanings: Altered form of   Fouts  , a surname of German origin.
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Fudge  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William la Zouche was born on 25 December 1321, in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, his father, Eudes la Zouche, was 24 and his mother, Joan Inge, was 23. He married Elizabeth de Ros on 16 July 1337, in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 23 April 1382, in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 60, and was buried in Biddlesden, Buckinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William la Zouche est né le 25 décembre 1321, à Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Eudes la Zouche, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Joan Inge, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth de Ros le 16 juillet 1337, à Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 23 avril 1382, à Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Biddlesden, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William la Zouche am 25. Dezember 1321 in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Eudes la Zouche 24 und seine Mutter Joan Inge 23. Er hat am 16. Juli 1337 in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England Elizabeth de Ros geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 23. April 1382 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Biddlesden, Buckinghamshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William la Zouche è nato il il 25 dicembre 1321 nel Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, il suo padre, Eudes la Zouche, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Joan Inge, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth de Ros il 16 luglio 1337, nel Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 23 aprile 1382, nel Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolto a Biddlesden, Buckinghamshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William la Zoucheが1321年12月25日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルスで生まれた時，父Eudes la Zoucheは24歳，母Joan Ingeは23歳であった。1337年7月16日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルスでElizabeth de Rosと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1382年4月23日にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルスで60歳で死去し，イギリスブクインガムシュアイアビッドレスデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William la Zouche(이)가 1321년 12월 25일 영국 노르섬프토실르 해링워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Eudes la Zouche(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Inge(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1337년 7월 16일 영국 노르섬프토실르 해링워스에서 Elizabeth de Ros(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1382년 4월 23일 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 해링워스에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 벅잉함실르 비들레즈든에 묻혔다.

pt.25 December 1321–23 April 1382: 25 de dezembro de 1321–23 de abril de 1382
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William la Zouche nasceu em 25 de dezembro de 1321, em Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Eudes la Zouche, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Joan Inge, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth de Ros em 16 de julho de 1337, em Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 23 de abril de 1382, em Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 60 anos, e foi sepultado em Biddlesden, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William la Zouche родился 25 декабря 1321 г. в местности харрингворт, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Eudes la Zouche было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Inge, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth de Ros 16 июля 1337 г. в местности харрингворт, нортамптоншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 23 апреля 1382 г. в местности харрингворт, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности биддлесден, буккингхамшир, Англия.

es.25 December 1321–23 April 1382: 25 de diciembre de 1321–23 de abril de 1382
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William la Zouche nació el 25 de diciembre de 1321, en Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Eudes la Zouche, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Joan Inge, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Elizabeth de Ros el 16 de julio de 1337, en Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 23 de abril de 1382, en Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultado en Biddlesden, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra.
